# Knowledge Hub — Hugo Lahutte (extended version)

> Hugo Lahutte's worklog and method — building his projects and content with Claude. Build in public.

Author: Hugo Lahutte — hugo@hl-consulting.tech — CEO of Cobra, publisher of Haute Fidélité, founder of HL Consulting.
Location: Chennevières-sur-Marne, Île-de-France, France.
Background: 17 years at Son-Vidéo.com (France's #1 HiFi/home cinema e-retailer) — purchasing, sales, commercial strategy, marketing/communications, partnerships; growth from €10M to €62M. Since 2024: CEO of Cobra (Oct. 2024), publisher & editorial director of Haute Fidélité (Jan. 2025, 30+ year reference magazine relaunched), founder of HL Consulting (Jul. 2024). Marketing lecturer at Creapole-ESDI. Passionate about music, cinema, and Japanese culture (Japan Expo 2004–2009).
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/hugo-lahutte — X: https://x.com/hugolh_ai

================================================================
METHOD: HOW HUGO LAHUTTE USES CLAUDE
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/how-to/
Published: 2026-05-30
================================================================

More and more people ask Hugo how he works with Claude.
Here's his setup — the one he passes along to friends when they get started.

1. WHAT ARE THESE FILES?
The core of the system is a set of .md (Markdown) files pasted into the
"Project Instructions" of a Claude project. Once that's done, every
conversation in that project starts with that context: Claude knows the
style, the industry, the ground rules. No need to re-explain yourself
every time.

2. THE PRINCIPLE: COMPOUND ENGINEERING
Every time Claude gets the style wrong, you add a rule. The file improves
with use and every future exchange benefits from it. The leverage isn't
a better one-off prompt — it's an asset that compounds over time.

3. HOW IT WORKS IN PRACTICE
- One main CLAUDE.md file: tone, response format, behavioral rules,
  professional context. Runs all the time.
- Extension files loaded on demand:
  - CLAUDE-strat.md — business / comms / marketing
  - CLAUDE-dev.md — code
  The main CLAUDE.md knows when to call on each one.
- A brand DNA file: history, positioning, tone, what you say
  and don't say. Loaded when Claude writes or communicates. ~1 hour
  to write, major impact on quality.

4. CLAUDE CHAT VS CLAUDE CODE
- Claude Chat (claude.ai): web interface, you create a Project and paste
  in the instructions. That's where the file system above lives.
- Claude Code: command-line tool in the terminal, inside the repo.
  It sees files, understands the codebase, executes code. 100% technical
  use. Same config principle.

5. WHERE TO START
   a. Create a Project on claude.ai.
   b. Ask Claude to build your CLAUDE.md with a scoped prompt
      (tone, format, professional context, rules, what you don't want,
      extension call logic; + a brand DNA file; method:
      one question at a time, section by section).
   c. Once the foundation is solid, write the brand DNA file.

PROMPT PROVIDED TO GENERATE YOUR CLAUDE.md:
"I want to create my CLAUDE.md file — a personal configuration file
for my Claude projects. [...] Method: ask me ONE question at a time.
We build it section by section. When we've covered everything, you
generate each complete file in markdown, separately."
(Full prompt on the how-to page.)

================================================================
GUIDES (EVERGREEN) — UNDERSTANDING AI TO MAKE DECISIONS
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/
================================================================

A series of clear guides on AI and the tools used, designed for merchants
and business owners who want to get up to speed fast — but readable at any
level. Each guide has three reading depths: "in 30 seconds", the visual body,
and "to go further".

GUIDE 01 — WHAT IS AI, AND WHAT'S AN "LLM"?
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/cest-quoi-ia-llm.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- AI vs LLM: "AI" is a broad term; an LLM (large language model) is a specific
  subset of it. Nesting: AI ⊃ Machine Learning ⊃ Deep Learning ⊃ LLM.
- Classical AI (specialized, coded for one task) vs generative AI (one system
  that writes, summarizes, translates, codes); the LLM is the text engine of
  generative AI.
- What an LLM is: a model trained on enormous volumes of text that predicts the
  most probable next token, one token at a time. It doesn't recite a database:
  it generates/reconstructs each response on the fly.
- The big players: OpenAI (ChatGPT), Anthropic (Claude), Google (Gemini),
  Meta (Llama, open weights), Mistral (French, sovereignty), DeepSeek
  (Chinese, open, performance/price), xAI (Grok), Microsoft (Copilot, strong
  on code), Perplexity (answer engine with cited sources). Versions change fast:
  think in terms of families.
- What an LLM can NOT do: it's neither a search engine nor a database — it can
  hallucinate (confidently invent falsehoods), so verify numbers/dates/references;
  by default it isn't connected to the real-time internet or your data (you have
  to connect it); it's a copilot, not an oracle.

GUIDE 02 — WHAT IS CLAUDE, AND WHY DID I BUILD EVERYTHING ON IT?
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/cest-quoi-claude.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Claude is Anthropic's AI assistant (American lab founded by ex-OpenAI
  researchers), with an obsession for reliability and safety.
- Not a single model but a family of three tiers: Opus (the powerhouse, complex
  tasks), Sonnet (the balanced one, everyday use), Haiku (the fast one, volume).
  Version numbers change; the logic stays.
- Why it's a top contender: professional AI code editors rely on Claude;
  Anthropic attracts the best (Andrej Karpathy, OpenAI co-founder, joined in
  May 2026); a model built for serious work (writing, analysis, reasoning, code).
- Why Hugo built everything on it: Cobra (management modules + technical SEO),
  Haute Fidélité (shop + logistics), daily automated hi-fi monitoring,
  and this Knowledge Hub — all shipped to production by a non-developer.

GUIDE 03 — WHY AI IS GENUINELY CHANGING THE WAY WE WORK
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/pourquoi-lia-change-le-travail.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Generative AI is a general-purpose technology, like electricity
  ("AI is the new electricity", Andrew Ng, 2017): it plugs into everything,
  across every industry.
- Signals that this isn't hype (examples current as of May 2026): founders
  are pivoting everything onto it (Jeff Bezos co-founded Project Prometheus),
  top-tier researchers are switching houses, giants are committing hundreds of
  billions per year to AI infrastructure.
- What it changes: work shifts from execution to oversight (describe and verify);
  a non-technical person can do technical things; the pace changes scale
  (weeks → hours).
- What it doesn't change: judgment stays human (AI proposes, you decide, it can
  be confidently wrong); value goes to those who frame the right problem and
  verify; the advantage belongs to early adopters who actually practice.

GUIDE 04 — CHAT, COWORK, OR CODE: WHICH ONE TO USE (AND THE "TERMINAL")
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/chat-cowork-code-terminal.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Three entry points to the same Claude: Chat (talk: think, write, analyze),
  Cowork (delegate office work autonomously, for non-developers), Code (act
  directly on files/projects/sites, run commands, deploy).
- The terminal is just a window where you type commands instead of clicking;
  no need to master it: copy-paste-confirm, and paste the error to Claude if
  something breaks.
- Installing Claude Code in one command: Mac/Linux "curl -fsSL
  https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash"; Windows (PowerShell) "irm
  https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex"; then "claude" (paid subscription).
  A Desktop app exists, no terminal needed. Docs: code.claude.com/docs/en/setup.
- No need for a powerful machine: all computing happens on Anthropic's servers.
  A good machine is useful for comfort and running automations (machine on 24/7).

GUIDE 05 — WHAT IS GITHUB, AND WHY IS IT FREE AND MAGICAL
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/cest-quoi-github.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- GitHub = an online place where your project lives, with the complete history
  of every change (a "Google Drive with total memory"): roll back to any version
  with one click, work without fear.
- Free for the essentials (owned by Microsoft; free tier sufficient for an
  indie/SME), and the world standard (any contractor knows how to use it).
- "Wire it once, keeps itself up to date": every pushed change deploys
  automatically online (no more FTP). Site, Shopify theme, Odoo automations
  all live there; Claude Code pushes, it's live ~1 min later.
- No need to master it: Claude Code handles versioning, the human validates.

GUIDE 06 — WHAT'S AN "MCP", AND HOW TO CONNECT CLAUDE TO YOUR TOOLS
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/cest-quoi-mcp-connecter-claude.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- By default Claude "can't see" your tools (a head with no hands). Connecting it
  means giving it hands.
- 4 levels, from simplest to most powerful: copy-paste / screenshot →
  browser → API → MCP.
- MCP = Model Context Protocol, an open standard created by Anthropic, like a
  "USB plug": any compatible tool connects without custom integration (Shopify,
  Drive, Gmail…), and Claude reads + acts within the authorized scope.
- In Hugo's setup: Shopify, Drive, emails connected; Claude Code linked to
  GitHub, which drives Odoo and Shopify. Claude works on real tools, not a demo.

GUIDE 07 — WHAT'S AN "AI AGENT"?
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/cest-quoi-un-agent-ia.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Assistant = responds (you ask, it answers, you take over). Agent = acts:
  autonomous loop goal → act → observe → adjust → deliver, with access to tools.
- Use cases: daily automated monitoring, email triage, metrics surveillance.
- Essential guardrail (lesson from an agent that burned its budget and produced
  nothing): explicit goal, spending cap, limited frequency, measurable success
  criterion.
- Start small: one agent, one task, prove reliability before expanding.

GUIDE 08 — WRITING A GOOD CLAUDE.MD (THE MOST VALUABLE FILE YOU'LL EVER MAKE)
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/ecrire-un-bon-claude-md.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- CLAUDE.md = a context file read at startup that sets tone, rules, traps:
  you stop repeating yourself, responses land right on the first try.
- Content (4 families): tone & format; conventions (industry, tools, vocabulary);
  off-limits (jargon, unsolicited plans, flattery); pitfalls → rules (the most
  valuable part).
- Best practices: short beats exhaustive; concrete beats abstract; one rule =
  one real mistake; split by context (base + extensions).
- Make it grow: every recurring error becomes a rule (Compound Engineering
  applied to your context).

GUIDE 09 — THE 4 PRINCIPLES I APPLY WITH AI (KARPATHY STYLE)
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/principes-facon-karpathy.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Andrej Karpathy: OpenAI co-founder, former head of AI at Tesla, great
  communicator (popularized "vibe coding"); joined Anthropic in May 2026.
- 4 principles (personal synthesis, in his spirit — not official rules):
  1) think before responding; 2) simplicity first; 3) surgical edits;
  4) goal-driven execution.
- Effect: save tokens, time, and quality; the "surgical" principle was born
  from a production deploy that broke everything (too many changes at once).

GUIDE 10 — COMPOUND ENGINEERING: YOUR METHOD IMPROVES ITSELF
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/compound-engineering.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Principle: turn every lesson into a reusable rule/tool; each unit of work
  makes the next one easier. Term popularized by Kieran Klaassen and Dan Shipper
  (Every) while building their email assistant.
- Loop: do → review → capture the lesson (it enters the context) →
  start again from a higher baseline.
- Examples: production deploy that breaks → hard rule; agent that burns its
  budget → budget guardrail. Incidents harden the system.
- Cumulative effect (compound interest): after dozens of sessions, the context
  is worth a team that knows you. That's what this Knowledge Hub is about.

GUIDE 11 — AI IN PRACTICE, FOR A MERCHANT OR AN SME
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/lia-pour-un-commercant.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- No need to be a tech company: AI hits the repetitive chores first —
  bulk product descriptions, SEO, cross-referencing sales (shop + back office),
  email triage, market monitoring.
- Gain: do in hours what used to take days, with the same headcount (not
  "replacing people").
- Doable without a tech team (non-developer shipping what used to need an agency);
  what matters: knowing what to ask for and verifying the output.
- Start with one precise, measurable, recurring chore; traps: AI makes mistakes
  (verify numbers), keep humans on customer relationships, don't over-automate
  what isn't broken.

GUIDE 12 — IS MY DATA SAFE WITH AI?
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/mes-donnees-sont-elles-en-securite.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Where data goes: to the provider's servers (cloud), encrypted in transit
  (HTTPS). By default an assistant doesn't "remember" across conversations
  (unless memory feature is on) and doesn't access your tools without a
  connection. The real question: who can see it and what does the provider do with it.
- Training: depends on the plan. Consumer (free) — conversations may be used to
  improve models, usually opt-outable in settings. Pro / enterprise / API —
  no training on your data by default, often contractual. Policies vary and
  evolve: check the "training" clause for your plan (Anthropic prioritizes
  privacy, but verify).
- Sovereignty / GDPR (for regulated or strategic data): EU hosting, a European
  provider like Mistral, or a self-hosted open model — more control, more
  complexity.
- 4 reflexes: 1) the right plan (pro for work); 2) disable training if the
  option exists; 3) no unnecessary secrets (anonymise); 4) sovereign solution
  reserved for sensitive data. General guidance, not legal advice.

GUIDE 13 — HOW TO TALK TO AI PROPERLY: WRITING A GOOD PROMPT
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/ecrire-un-bon-prompt.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- A prompt = the instruction you give AI; it's the single biggest driver of
  results. No "magic formula": clarity wins.
- Recipe for a good prompt: context (who/situation) + precise task + output
  format + constraints (length, tone, what to avoid), and one example of the
  desired result.
- Reflexes: give an example, iterate (say what's off), ask AI to ask its own
  questions, give it a role, break a big request down.
- When you keep typing the same instructions every time, lock them into a
  CLAUDE.md.

GUIDE 14 — BEING VISIBLE IN AI: GEO EXPLAINED
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/etre-visible-dans-lia-geo.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- SEO = being found on a search engine (list of links, you're after the click).
  GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) = being cited in the response written
  by an AI (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, AI Overviews).
- Why it matters: more and more people ask an AI instead of Googling; if it
  doesn't cite you, you don't exist in that response — even if you rank well on
  Google.
- How to get cited: Q&A structure, factual and citable statements, structured
  data (schema.org, FAQPage), identifiable entity (author, authority), llms.txt
  file. Overlaps heavily with good SEO.
- Emerging and opaque field: no guarantees, but solid principles. This site is
  built as an example (1 page/question, JSON-LD, llms.txt/full).

GUIDE 15 — HOW MUCH DOES AI COST: SUBSCRIPTIONS, API, AND KEEPING THE BILL UNDER CONTROL
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/combien-coute-lia.html
Published: 2026-05-31

- Everything is counted in tokens (chunks of words read + produced): the longer
  the exchange, the more it costs; a powerful model costs more than a fast one.
- 4 pricing models: free (limited, data possibly used); personal subscription
  (flat fee, often ~€20/month); pay-per-use API (per token, for automating);
  enterprise (per seat, with guarantees). Prices evolve: think in orders of
  magnitude.
- Subscription for daily conversations; API for automating / connecting tools /
  agents.
- Controlling the bill: spending caps on the API, the right model for the task,
  scoped agents (budget, frequency). Example: the daily hi-fi monitoring runs on
  the API for a few euros/month because it's well-scoped.

================================================================
BUILD IN PUBLIC — CASE STUDIES (GUIDE CLUSTER)
================================================================

PILLAR GUIDE — A SITE + AN AI BOT IN A WEEKEND?
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/site-bot-ia-en-un-week-end.html
Published: 2026-06-01

Question: can a non-developer ship a real site + an AI bot in a weekend?
Answer: YES. Without knowing how to code, two real things in production with
Claude Code: a complete static site (journal, guides, glossary, search, dark
mode, GEO) on GitHub Pages; a serverless AI monitoring bot that summarizes
the news and publishes it every morning on Telegram + on the site. The secret
isn't technical: make good decisions (simple architecture, zero dependencies,
clear data contract) and let AI hit the code while you verify the output and
the facts. Body: 1) start with the need, not the tech; 2) dumbest possible
stack (vanilla HTML/CSS/JS + GitHub Pages; Python script + GitHub Actions);
3) separate responsibilities (bot = engine, site = display, connected by a
single feed.json, secrets isolated); 4) visual loop with AI; 5) guardrails
(budget, copyright, security). Key takeaway: the blocker is no longer "I can't
code" — deciding, scoping, verifying is exactly what a business owner already
knows how to do.

CASE STUDY — KNOWLEDGE HUB: FROM IDEA TO PRODUCTION
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/etude-de-cas-knowledge-hub.html
Published: 2026-06-01

Honest framing: doesn't cover creation from scratch (pre-existing base: 43
journal entries, 4 verticals, search, encrypted vault, "HiFi Lovers" design
system). Covers the editorial + UX layer added: evergreen guides, glossary,
dark mode, nav overhaul, GEO layer. Stack decided early, partly on AI's advice:
simplicity / zero build, free (GitHub Pages), no framework. Decisions: 3-depth
guides; visuals/logos coded in inline HTML/CSS/SVG; thematic cluster on the
landing page; CSS token dark mode [data-theme]; anti-FOUC via ~3-line inline
script; verified facts, never model version numbers; custom favicon/logo.
Struggles: FOUC (inline script in the head); iOS cache (cache-busting ?v=);
parallel sessions on main (resync + idempotent scripts); untouchable encrypted
vault. Time/cost: 3 calendar days (May 30 → June 1, 2026), ~70 commits,
most of it on Saturday (several effective hours, in parallel with Cobra project).
Zero token cost (Claude subscription). It's TIME we're talking about here.
Illustrates: no framework needed; AI shifts the bottleneck (deciding + verifying);
honesty is a feature.

CASE STUDY — AI MONITORING BOT: FROM IDEA TO PRODUCTION (SERVERLESS)
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/etude-de-cas-veille-ia.html
Published: 2026-06-01

An agent that retrieves AI news, has it summarized by the Claude API, and
publishes it on Telegram + the "AI Monitor" page of the site, serverlessly,
orchestrated by GitHub Actions. Pipeline: X/Twitter accounts + RSS (feeds.txt)
→ twitterapi.io → Claude API (selection + neutral FR summary, anti-hype, imposed
JSON Schema; factual fields re-read from source, not entrusted to the model) →
two outputs from the same collection: Telegram (daily push) and feed.json →
site (archive). Triggered every morning (05:00 UTC). Modules: twitter.py,
feed.py, digest.py, telegram.py, main.py, backfill.py. Decisions: two separate
repos (veille-ia engine + secrets; knowledge-hub public site with no secrets);
reading feed.json at runtime; categorization by tags; transformative summaries —
never a copied tweet; X embed + image+link fallback. Stack: twitterapi.io
(~cents/day), RSS, Claude API (claude-opus-4-8, adjustable to claude-sonnet-4-6),
Telegram Bot API (t.me/VeilleIA_HL), GitHub Actions (cron 1 run/day), GitHub +
GitHub Pages. feed.json = cumulative data contract; seen.json = deduplication;
cross-repo bridge via dedicated PAT. No secret ever published (placeholders).
Struggles: cross-repo bridge (add/add conflicts → reset --hard on the source of
truth + 3 attempts); Telegram blocking everything (non-blocking optional send);
divergent secret name (alias); secrets.* forbidden in a workflow if: condition
(guardrail moved to the script); unbalanced backfill (quota per account +
global cap). Cost: twitterapi.io €100 prepaid "to test the waters" (comfort
cap, real consumption ~cents/day); Claude API a handful of cents/day; GitHub
Actions free. Strict budget guardrails. Illustrates: a useful agent fits in
~300 lines and 0 servers; the right architecture prevents the headaches; keep
both the human and the LLM honest.

MAKING-OF — SHIPPED IN A WEEKEND
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/guides/making-of.html
Published: 2026-06-01

Friday May 29, 2026, dinner with friends: needed one place to show what I'm
building with AI. Saturday morning, launched the site, everything shipped to
production in sequence: journal, glossary, tags, search, dark mode (72 pages),
AI Monitor page, and a bot (AI news → Claude API → Telegram + site), all the
way to a custom favicon/logo. A beautiful thing shipped in ~2 days, without
being a developer, side by side with Claude Code. Two documented and costed
use cases: the site (zero token cost) and the AI monitoring bot (~cents/day).
Next: LinkedIn announcement, Telegram channel documentation, public follow-up.

================================================================
AI GLOSSARY — JARGON-FREE DEFINITIONS
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/lexique/
================================================================
Glossary of AI terms, the single source of truth for the site's tooltips.

- Artificial intelligence (AI): Software capable of tasks that used to require a human brain (understanding, deciding, creating). "Classical AI" is specialized; generative AI is versatile.
- Generative AI: An AI that produces new content (text, image, code) from a simple instruction, instead of executing a single programmed task.
- LLM (large language model): The engine behind assistants like Claude or ChatGPT: a model trained on enormous amounts of text that predicts the most plausible continuation of what you type.
- Token: The basic unit an LLM reads and produces: a chunk of a word. The length of exchanges and their cost are measured in tokens.
- Hallucination: When an LLM confidently invents a false answer. Hence the basic rule: verify facts, numbers, and references.
- Prompt: The instruction you give the AI. A good prompt — clear, with context and goal — changes everything about the result.
- Multimodal: Capable of processing multiple types of input — text, image, audio — not just text.
- Open weights: A model you can download and self-host (e.g. Llama, Mistral), as opposed to "closed" models accessible only via a service.
- Anthropic: The American lab that creates Claude, focused on model reliability and safety.
- Claude: Anthropic's AI assistant, available in multiple sizes (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) and multiple products (Chat, Cowork, Code).
- Opus / Sonnet / Haiku: Claude's three tiers: the most powerful (Opus), the balanced (Sonnet), the fast one (Haiku). You pick based on the task.
- Claude Chat / Cowork / Code: Three ways to use Claude: chat (Chat), delegate office work (Cowork), act on files and projects (Code).
- Terminal: The window where you type commands instead of clicking. Looks scary, but it runs on copy-paste.
- API: A programmed pipe connecting two pieces of software. It's one of the ways to connect a service to Claude, in an automated way.
- MCP (Model Context Protocol): An open standard created by Anthropic: a "universal plug" to connect Claude to tools (Shopify, Drive, Gmail…) without custom integration.
- AI agent: Claude given a goal and the means to act on its own, in a loop, until it delivers — instead of just responding.
- GitHub: An online space where your project lives with its full history; once connected, updates deploy themselves.
- Repository (repo): The "folder" of a project on GitHub, with all its files and history.
- Commit: A saved, timestamped version of a project on GitHub. You can always go back to it.
- Cron: A timer that launches a task automatically at regular intervals (e.g. every morning at 7am).
- RSS: A standard format for automatically pulling new articles from a site. The basic building block of any monitoring setup.
- Bot / Telegram channel: A program that posts messages on Telegram; a public channel is followable by anyone via a simple link.
- CLAUDE.md: A file Claude reads at startup: tone, conventions, pitfalls. It saves you from re-explaining everything every conversation.
- Compound Engineering: A method (popularized by Kieran Klaassen and Dan Shipper, at Every) where each lesson becomes a reusable rule: your system improves itself with every session.
- Vibe coding: Programming by describing your intent in plain language and letting AI generate the code. Term popularized by Andrej Karpathy.
- SEO: The art of ranking well in search engines (Google…).
- GEO: The SEO equivalent for AI: optimizing your content to be **cited** by assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity). That's the goal of this site.
- OpenAI / ChatGPT: The American lab behind ChatGPT, the assistant that kicked off the consumer generative AI wave in late 2022.
- Google / Gemini: Google's AI, integrated into its ecosystem and known for long documents and multimodal capabilities (text, image, audio).
- Meta / Llama: Meta's family of "open weights" models: you can download and self-host them.
- Mistral: France's and Europe's AI champion, partly open weights. The sovereignty argument when hosting your data in Europe matters.
- DeepSeek: The Chinese open model, formidable on the performance/price ratio — but raising data privacy questions.
- xAI / Grok: Elon Musk's model (xAI), wired directly into the X social network.
- Microsoft / Copilot: Microsoft's AI, integrated into Windows and the Office suite, and widely used for code. Under the hood: partner models (including OpenAI).
- Perplexity: Less a model than an "answer engine": it searches the web and responds with cited sources.
================================================================
ABOUT — HUGO LAHUTTE
URL: https://hugolahutte.github.io/knowledge-hub/en/a-propos/
================================================================
High-end audio/video entrepreneur. A coherent ecosystem: Cobra
(HiFi & Home Cinema retail, Odoo/Shopify tech stack), Haute Fidélité
(reference magazine relaunched in 2025), HiFi Lovers (community brand).
Common thread: one consistent way of working with Claude applied everywhere,
and a life of passion (music, cinema, design, pop culture) that feeds the brands.

================================================================
PROJECTS
================================================================
- Cobra — tech stack, Odoo ERP, Shopify sync, automations,
  driven with a dedicated Claude Code account.
- Haute Fidélité — high-end audio electronics boutique.
- HiFi Lovers — neo-retro audiophile brand (design, merch, editorial).
- Personal — side projects and experiments.

Coherence: dev (Cobra) feeds the shops, shops feed the community
(HiFi Lovers), personal projects serve as the lab.

================================================================
JOURNAL
================================================================
Dated entries, one HTML page per session. Two Claude agents contribute:
- Claude · HL (personal): strategy sessions, personal projects.
- Claude · Cobra (dev): Odoo / Shopify / dev sessions.

================================================================
CONTACT
================================================================
Hugo documents his practice in public and is open to conversations about
Claude, his method, and his projects. No commercial pitch for now —
just happy to talk. Contact: hugo@hl-consulting.tech.
